purpose: Pinned external Lean formalizations relevant to the atlas. Each record states the machine-checked scope precisely; it does not alter erdosproblems.com status or assert prize eligibility.

records.title: Obligatory triple systems
claims: For every finite triple system F, F.IsObligatory ↔ F.isolatedReduction.Intrinsic.
For every finite triple system F, F.IsObligatory ↔ Constructible F.isolatedReduction.
replay: git clone https://github.com/SamPetkov/Erdos593.git && cd Erdos593 && git checkout d834f1f42e7e0d5d2a6c23e9487563bedb1b277b
cd formalization && lake build && python scripts/generate_self_contained.py --check && lake env lean Erdos593SelfContained.lean
records.trust_boundary: The pin records kernel checking of the stated finite classification. It does not establish historical priority, independent discovery, external peer review, or a change to the canonical problem-page status.

attribution.related_work: Eric Li, arXiv:2606.24882. The pinned repository describes Li's contemporaneous preprint as proving the same classification, using a one-apex sequence lift in greater cardinal generality and related bridge-decomposition ideas; it is cited for comparison and is not a logical premise of the Lean development.

records.title: Chromatic–cochromatic gap in G(n,1/2)
claims: The checkpoint includes the finite Section VII falling-factorial ratio, prefix-local full-corner induction, and complementary-profile sum reindexing lemmas.
The project explicitly leaves Erdos625Statement and the manuscript's Sections VIII–IX asymptotic/probabilistic assemblies unproved.
replay: git clone https://github.com/SamPetkov/Erdos.git && cd Erdos && git checkout e03159d09ddf019196d81a647632c913230fb1cf
cd 625/formalization && python scripts/generate_self_contained.py --check && lake build --wfail && lake env lean -DwarningAsError=true Erdos625SelfContained.lean
records.trust_boundary: This is a scoped Lean checkpoint, not a formal proof of Erdős Problem 625, not evidence that the currently open canonical problem is solved, and not a prize claim.

attribution.related_work: No external coauthorship or priority claim is made by this registry.
